english: (समाधि Trigl., Was. also समापत्ति) contemplation, profound meditation, perfect absorption of mind, cf. bsam-gtán (bsam gtan), and sgóm-pa (sgom pa); tiṅ-ṅe-°dzín byéd-pa (ting nge 'dzin byed pa) Sch., gen. tiṅ-ṅe-°dzín-du jùg-pa (ting nge 'dzin du 'jug pa) to be absorbed in deep meditation; tiṅ-ṅe-°dzin °kruṅ (ting nge 'dzin 'khrung) devout meditation takes place; also meton.: the faculty, the power of meditating e.g. pél (phel) Mil.
Sik. shrew(-mouse).
clean, well-swept Ld., Ts.
snipe Ld.
little bells moved by the wind Sch.
resp. gsol-tíb (gsol tib), tea-pot, zaṅs-tíb (zangs tib) a copper tea-pot, *rdza-tib* (rdza tib) an earthen tea-pot.
Mil. goat's leather, kid-leather, from India, dyed green or blue.
Ts. funnel.
(तिल) sesame, til-már (til mar) sesame-oil, seed-oil.
1. num. fig.: 69. — 2. an affix, denoting the terminative case, or the direction to a place, joined to the final consonants g and b; cf. du (du), ru (ru), su (su).
(Turk. توپک) W. gun, musket, fire-lock, fowling-piece, *gyáb-ċe* (gyab ce) to discharge, fire off; *tú-pag-man* (tu pag man) gun-powder.
Ma. the Turks, Turkomans, तुरुष्क, ترک.
v. ti-tsa (ti tsa).
(Ssk., Hind.) a balance, pair of scales, C. *tú-la tég-pa* (tu la teg pa) to weigh.
'cannot' Sch. (?)
or tug-čúm (tug chum), prob. also tug-čém (tug chem), Cs.: a wooden rattle's sound or noise; Sch. also: the trotting of horses heard in the distance; tug-riṅ-ċàn (tug ring can) Stg. noisy (?).
tub (tub), tub gcig-tu rgyud-do? (tub gcig tu rgyud do?)
Lex. w.e.; Sch.: 'either — or, whether I be able (to do it) or not' (?).
(?) W. to darn (stockings).

clear, distinct, syn. to wál-le (wal le); yid túr-re °dug (yid tur re 'dug) it is clear to my mind; túr-re bzuṅ (tur re bzung) Mil. prob. watch it! have a sharp eye upon it! że-sdáṅ (zhe sdang) laṅs dogs túr-re gyis (tur re gyis) Mil. take care lest an emotion of anger arise in your mind! túr-gyis sad (tur gyis sad) Mil prob.: he awakes, stirs, is evidently roused; túr-re-ba (tur re ba) Glr.; adj. (or abstract noun), rjed-yeṅ-méd-par túr-re-ba (rjed yeng med par tur re ba) clear, firm with regard to perceptions, opinions etc., without omission or digression.

1. num. fig.: 99. — 2. an affix denoting the gerund, and used after the final letters n, r, l, s (v. Grammar), to be translated by the participle in ing, or sentences beginning with when, after, as etc.; also used as a finite tense (though seldom in B.), and in that case followed by °dug ('dug) or yod (yod), or also without these words: *dád-de °dug* (dad de 'dug) I sit W.; gró-ba yin-te (gro ba yin te) Mil. I go.
Lex. = légs-par (legs par); Sch.: very, really, actually.
Sch. constantly, continually.
v. ti-se (ti se).

imp. tog (tog), C., W., = °tégs-pa ('tegs pa), to pack up, put up; to put in or into, *°am-bág-la* ('am bag la) into one's bosom; *tag-t'ul* (tag thul) or *t'ug* (thug) preparations for a journey, *taṅ-ċe* (tang ce) W. to make.
? Ld.-Glr. (Schl. f. 25, b); teu sér-po (te'u ser po); Mil. 59, 4 of my edition; Lex.: teu śiṅ-kri (te'u shing khri), where Sch. translates: a square table.
Cs.: an instrument for burning Med.; lċágs-tel (lcags tel) such an instrument of iron Cs.; sprá-tel (spra tel) Lt.?
1. num for 129 — 2. affix added to certain verbs, when they terminate a sentence.
W., an adv. denoting a swinging motion;
